data.author: Senior Airman Erin Currie
data.title: 18th LRS Deployment and Distribution Flight Keeps Kadena’s Mission Moving [Image 1 of 4]
data.description: U.S. Air Force Airman 1st Class Zoe Cathey, 18th Logistics Readiness Squadron inbound cargo technician, measures cargo for a deployed unit at Kadena Air Base, Japan, Sept 26, 2025. Cargo technicians inspect every piece of in and outbound cargo assigned to deployed units to ensure proper measurements are taken and that the equipment is prepared for transport. (U.S. Air photo by Senior Airman Erin Currie)

data.author: Senior Airman Erin Currie
data.title: Refueled and Ready: U.S. and Japan enhance ACE capabilities at Kadena [Image 2 of 4]
data.description: U.S. Air Force Senior Airman Christian Morales, 18th Logistics Readiness Squadron deployment technician, communicates with fellow deployment technicians during a cargo inspection for a deployed unit at Kadena Air Base, Japan, Sept 26, 2025. The team responsible for coordinating with Deployed Force Employment units on Kadena, known as Task Force Jumanji, is made up of multiple AFSCs working together to streamline the deployment process and ensure every unit sent to Kadena is well taken care of. (U.S. Air Force photo by Senior Airman Erin Currie)

data.title: 18th LRS Deployment and Distribution Flight Keeps Kadena’s Mission Moving [Image 3 of 4]
data.description: U.S. Air Force Airman 1st Class Jarod Peyton, 18th Logistics Readiness Squadron Ground Transportation support operator, waits for deployed service members arriving at Kadena Air Base, Japan, Sept 26, 2025. data.title: 18th LRS Deployment and Distribution Flight Keeps Kadena’s Mission Moving [Image 4 of 4]
data.description: U.S. Air Force Staff Sgt. Barbara Wojtasinski, 18th Logistics Readiness personal property supervisor, left, and Senior Airman Kaleb Hubbard, 18th Logistics Readiness Squadron passenger travel technician, check in deployed members preparing for departure from Kadena Air Base, Japan, Sept. 30, 2025. data.title: Military working dogs take on EOD [Image 7 of 7]
data.description: Kkody, 380th Expeditionary Security Forces Squadron military working dog, awaits instruction from U.S. Air Force Senior Airman Ryan Longoria, 380th ESFS MWD handler, within the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility, Nov. 5, 2025. data.title: Military working dogs take on EOD [Image 3 of 7]
data.description: U.S. Air Force Senior Airman Ryan Longoria, 380th Expeditionary Security Force Squadron military working dog handler, grabs Kkody, 380th ESFS MWD, while he bites Senior Airman Bryan Matthews, 1st Expeditionary Theater Support Group explosive ordnance disposal team member, within the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility, Nov. 5, 2025. data.title: Military working dogs take on EOD [Image 2 of 7]

data.title: Military working dogs take on EOD [Image 1 of 7]
data.description: U.S. Air Force Senior Airman Bryan Matthews, 1st Expeditionary Theater Support Group explosive ordnance disposal team member, right, braces for Senior Airman Ryan Longoria, 380th Expeditionary Security Force Squadron military working dog handler, to release Kkody, 380th ESFS MWD, to bite him within the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility, Nov. 5, 2025. data.title: Pakistan Edible Oil Imports Surge in 2025
data.description: Pakistan edible oil imports are expected to reach 3.5 million metric tonnes in 2025 as domestic demand rises. Westbury Group CEO Abdul Rasheed Jan Mohammad said population growth and economic development drive this increase. From January to October, imports already hit 3.07 million tonnes, exceeding last year’s total. Analysts said rising consumption pressures both supply […]

data.author: Sinisa Markovic
data.title: Protecting mobile privacy in real time with predictive adversarial defense
data.description: Mobile sensors are everywhere, quietly recording how users move, tilt, or hold their phones. The same data that powers step counters and activity trackers can also expose personal details such as gender, age, or even identity. A new study introduces a method designed to stop that information from being inferred in the first place, without interrupting the phone’s normal functions. Overview of the PATN framework. The system includes PATN training, where the network learns to … More →The post Protecting mobile privacy in real time with predictive adversarial defense appeared first...

data.author: Senior Airman Paige Weldon
data.title: 380th ELRS offloads cargo within CENTCOM [Image 2 of 6]
data.description: U.S. Airmen with the 380th Expeditionary Logistics Readiness Squadron prepare to offload cargo within the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility, Nov. 5, 2025. The Airmen were a part of the Air Terminal Operations Center and were responsible for on and off loading cargo and passengers from aircraft. (U.S. Air Force photo by Senior Airman Paige Weldon)

data.author: Senior Airman Paige Weldon
data.title: 380th ELRS offloads cargo within CENTCOM [Image 6 of 6]
data.description: U.S. Airmen with the 380th Expeditionary Logistics Readiness Squadron offload U.S. Army MIM-104 Patriot surface-to-air-missile system from a C-17 Globemaster III aircraft within the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility, Nov. 5, 2025. Multiple pieces of equipment comprise the Patriot missile system, enabling it to accomplish its communication, command and control, radar surveillance, and missile guidance missions. (U.S. Air Force photo by Senior Airman Paige Weldon)

data.author: Senior Airman Paige Weldon
data.title: 380th ELRS offloads cargo within CENTCOM [Image 1 of 6]
data.description: A U.S. Airman with the 380th Expeditionary Logistics Readiness Squadron guides a forklift to remove cargo from an aircraft within the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility, Nov. 5, 2025. The Airman ensured the path of the forklift remained clear and that the cargo was safely removed. (U.S. Air Force photo by Senior Airman Paige Weldon)

data.author: Senior Airman Paige Weldon
data.title: 380th ELRS offloads cargo within CENTCOM [Image 5 of 6]
data.description: U.S. Air Force Staff Sgt. John Catalla, 16th Expeditionary Airlift Squadron loadmaster, directs a forklift to offload cargo off a C-17 Globemaster III aircraft within the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility, Nov. 5, 2025. The forklift was operated by a member of the 380th Expeditionary Logistics Readiness Squadron Air Terminal Operations Center which handles on and offloading of cargo from aircraft. (U.S. Air Force photo by Senior Airman Paige Weldon)

data.author: Senior Airman Paige Weldon
data.title: 380th ELRS offloads cargo within CENTCOM [Image 3 of 6]
data.description: U.S. Airmen with the 380th Expeditionary Logistics Readiness Squadron approach a C-17 Globemaster III aircraft to offload cargo within the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility, Nov. 5, 2025. The Airmen communicated with a loadmaster aboard the C-17 to safely offload the cargo. (U.S. Air Force photo by Senior Airman Paige Weldon)
